title: Donatello, St. Mark (quiz)
topic_path: Sculpture and architecture in central Italy

assessment_items.question_content:
**Donatello's Saint Mark originally stood in a niche in the exterior of [[☃ dropdown 1]] and was commissioned by the linen workers' guild.**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f0fda5cd4550f1fae02fc06bf7ee952d.jpg)
hints:
**Donatello's Saint Mark originally stood in a niche in the exterior of Orsanmichele and was commissioned by the linen workers' guild.**

**[Watch this video to review](https://www.khanacademy.org/humanities/art-history/art-history-1400-1500-renaissance-in-italy-and-the-north/florence-1/v/donatello--st--mark--1411-13)**

assessment_items.question_content:
**St. Mark's face is highly individualized with a lot of detail, showing the influence of medieval spirituality.**



![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f0fda5cd4550f1fae02fc06bf7ee952d.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**St. Mark's face is highly individualized with a lot of detail, showing the influence of Renaissance Humanism.**

**[Watch this video to review](https://www.khanacademy.org/humanities/art-history/art-history-1400-1500-renaissance-in-italy-and-the-north/florence-1/v/donatello--st--mark--1411-13)**

assessment_items.question_content:
**In the figure of *St. Mark*, Donatello revived several classical features that were important to later Renaissance sculpture, including . . .**



![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f0fda5cd4550f1fae02fc06bf7ee952d.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**This figure revives a number of classical features that were important to later Renaissance sculpture, including contrapposto and revealing the body through the drapery.**

**[Watch this video to review](https://www.khanacademy.org/humanities/art-history/art-history-1400-1500-renaissance-in-italy-and-the-north/florence-1/v/donatello--st--mark--1411-13)**

choices.content: revealing the body through the drapery.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is NOT true of Donatello's St. Mark and its location in Orsanmichele?**



![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f0fda5cd4550f1fae02fc06bf7ee952d.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**The bottom of the niche was above most viewer's heads so that they had to look up and see St. Mark.**

**[Watch this video to review](https://www.khanacademy.org/humanities/art-history/art-history-1400-1500-renaissance-in-italy-and-the-north/florence-1/v/donatello--st--mark--1411-13)**

choices.content: Florence's guilds commissioned statues for the exterior niches of Orsanmichele, which was a granary and market.
This figure was separate from his niche, unlike medieval sculpture that blended niches and sculpture.
Donatello studied classical sculpture and the way that ancient artists represented the body, but also imbued St. Mark with distinctly human behavior.
The bottom of the niche was near the ground so that St. Mark would be eye-level with the viewer.
